Dog Policy: Dogs allowed in parking lot only. Not allowed on temple grounds or inside buildings.
Activities: guided temple tours · visitor center · reflecting pool viewing · Chinese New Year celebration · Lion Dance performances
Notes: Tours Thu–Sun 10am–5pm only. No trails in park itself. Oldest Chinese temple in CA.

Dog Policy: Dogs on leash allowed in parking lots and paved roadways only. Not allowed inside buildings or on unpaved grounds.
Activities: historic ruins exploration · courthouse museum · interpretive exhibits · walking/hiking on grounds · photography · gold rush history tour
Notes: 1850s gold-rush boomtown ruins. Closed Tue–Wed. Haunting atmosphere.

Dog Policy: Dogs on leash allowed in picnic areas and parking lots. Not allowed inside buildings or exhibit areas.
Activities: picnicking (Sacramento River views) · historic adobe tour · blacksmith shop viewing · pioneer demonstrations · StoryWalk · holiday programs
Notes: Adobe home of Bear Flag Republic's only president. Small but charming. Hours limited — call ahead.

Dog Policy: Policy unknown — mansion closed since December 2024 fire. Contact park at (530) 895-6144.
Activities: guided mansion tours (pre-fire) · interpretive exhibits · visitor center · walking grounds
Notes: CLOSED since December 2024 fire — verify reopening before visiting! Adjacent Bidwell Park has excellent cycling and hiking.

Top Hike (Name): Malakoff Diggins & South Yuba Trail Loop
Dog Policy: Dogs on leash (max 6 ft) allowed on hiking trails, in historic town, campground, and cabins. Not allowed inside historic buildings.
Activities: hiking (20+ miles) · camping · swimming (Blair Lake) · fishing (Blair Lake) · ghost town tours · guided gold panning (Saturdays 3pm) · visitor center · snowshoeing (winter) · picnicking
Notes: All-paved route available — 26 mi / ~50 min from Nevada City via Tyler Foote Rd. Largest hydraulic gold mine in world. Spectacular landscape.

Dog Policy: Dogs on leash (max 6 ft) allowed on roads, trails, and historic zone. Not allowed inside historic buildings or mine shafts. Water available for dogs.
Activities: hiking (14 miles) · mountain biking · mine shaft tour · garden walks · guided tours · visitor center/exhibits · picnicking · birdwatching
Notes: 14 miles of trails including mountain biking. Largest, deepest gold mine in CA history. Excellent museum.

Dog Policy: Dogs on leash allowed on most trails and in the river area. Not allowed inside buildings, on Monument Trail, or Monroe Ridge Trail.
Activities: gold panning (lessons + self-guided) · hiking · historic building tours · sawmill replica viewing · museum · swimming/wading (American River) · picnicking · living history events
Notes: Site of 1848 gold discovery. Gold panning available. Monroe Ridge Trail has great views. Living history programs on July 4th.

Dog Policy: Dogs on leash allowed on walking paths and grounds near Lake Natoma.
Activities: historic powerhouse tours (by reservation) · walking paths · lake views · interactive electricity exhibits · guided monthly events · photography
Notes: First long-distance hydroelectric transmission site. Tours only on weekends. Connects to 32-mile American River bike trail.

Cycling: American River Parkway Bike Trail (23 mi)
Dog Policy: No confirmed policy — primarily an indoor museum. Call ahead: (916) 324-0971.
Activities: self-guided museum tours · hands-on exhibits (pump drill / mortar & pestle) · cultural artifact viewing · interpretive programs · special events
Notes: Urban museum adjacent to Sutter's Fort. No hiking on-site. American River Parkway bike trail 1.9 mi away. Open daily 10am–5pm.

Top Hike (Name): Indian Grinding Rock South Nature Trail
Dog Policy: Dogs on leash (max 6 ft) allowed on paved roads and walkway, and at campsite. Not allowed on unpaved trails, open meadows, or forested areas.
Activities: hiking (1-mile North Trail loop) · camping · roundhouse viewing · Miwok village site visit · museum visit · bedrock mortar viewing · cultural programs · picnicking
Notes: Largest collection of bedrock mortars in North America (1185 holes). Bark house camping available. Miwok cultural village on site.

Dog Policy: Dogs on 6-ft leash allowed in parking lot, North Flat section, and Ranch House Yard picnic area. Not allowed on trails or in Natural Preserve.
Activities: hiking · birdwatching · kayaking/paddling · fishing · picnicking · guided ranch house tours · archaeological site viewing
Notes: Tule marsh on Clear Lake. 24 prehistoric Koi sites up to 14000 yrs old. Bird watching destination.

Dog Policy: Dogs are NOT permitted anywhere in this park. Only service animals excepted.
Activities: hiking (including Mt. St. Helena summit at 4343 ft) · trail running · birdwatching · photography · nature study
Notes: Strenuous summit hike (2119 ft gain) — one of the best in the Bay Area. No facilities or water at trailhead. Free entry. RLS honeymooned here in 1880.

Dog Policy: Dogs are not allowed on trails or in historic buildings or gardens. Only service animals permitted.
Activities: milling demonstrations (weekends) · guided historic tours · hiking (trail connects to Bothe-Napa Valley SP) · picnicking · flour/cornmeal purchase
Notes: 1846 water-powered grist mill. Trail connects to Bothe-Napa Valley SP. Mill demonstrations on weekends. $8 adults. Sat–Sun 10am–4pm.

Cycling: Mountain biking on 26 miles of trails
Dog Policy: Dogs on leash allowed in Beauty Ranch area and on trail to Wolf House Ruins. Not allowed on backcountry trails or beyond the silos.
Activities: hiking (26+ miles) · mountain biking · horseback riding · birdwatching · picnicking · Wolf House ruins tour · House of Happy Walls Museum · Beauty Ranch tour
Notes: 1400 acres. 26 miles of trails including MTB and equestrian. Beauty Ranch ruins and cottage. Sonoma Mountain summit (2400 ft). $10/car.

Dog Policy: Dogs on leash allowed in historic zone and developed paved areas. Not allowed on dirt trails or in historic buildings.
Activities: historic site walking tour · Mission San Francisco Solano visit · Sonoma Barracks tour · Toscano Hotel viewing · General Vallejo home tour · picnicking · living history events
Notes: Mission-era barracks and Bear Flag Republic site on Sonoma Plaza. Multiple historic buildings. Combine with Jack London for full day. Free entry.

Scuba Nearby: YES — SS Pomona shipwreck; Fort Ross Cove reef
Dog Policy: Dogs on leash allowed in parking lots, roads, picnic areas, and campgrounds. Not allowed on trails, beaches, or inside the Fort compound.
Activities: historic fort tour · visitor center/museum · shore fishing (license required) · kayaking · scuba diving (SS Pomona wreck) · surfing · camping · picnicking · cultural events
Notes: 1812 Russian settlement. Campground open Apr–Nov, first-come first-served, $40/night. Scuba: SS Pomona wreck (certified divers) and Fort Ross Cove reef.

Dog Policy: Dogs on leash allowed in outdoor areas including trails and historic grounds. Not allowed inside the Adobe building or visitor center.
Activities: self-guided adobe tours · guided docent tours · picnicking · living history events · craft demonstrations · historical reenactments · interpretive exhibits
Notes: General Vallejo's enormous 1836 rancho headquarters. Very short trail but historic grounds worth an hour. Wed–Sun 10am–5pm. $3/car.

Dog Policy: Dogs on leash allowed in historic ranch area only. Not permitted on park trails.
Activities: hiking · horseback riding · picnicking · historic site viewing (Miwok structures / Camilo Ynitia adobe) · birdwatching · wildflower viewing
Notes: Coast Miwok site inhabited 6000+ years. Grateful Dead lived here 1966–67. Can extend to Mt Burdell for 8-mi loop. Check hours — limited open days. $8/car.

Cycling: 2 mi paved bike path in park
Dog Policy: Dogs on leash (max 6 ft) allowed on paved paths and Dillon Point fishing area. Not allowed on marsh trails.
Activities: hiking · cycling · fishing (sturgeon / striped bass / starry flounder) · birdwatching · picnicking · rollerblading/skating · equestrian use
Notes: California's 2nd state capital 1853–54. 720 acres of marsh and hills. SF Bay Trail access. No tent camping — enroute motorhome sites only.

Dog Policy: Dogs on leash allowed in outdoor areas and on open observation cars of steam train excursions.
Activities: steam train excursion rides (weekends Apr–Oct) · roundhouse tours · movie prop tours · interpretive center exhibits · gift shop · special events
Notes: Historic steam railroad — no hiking trails. Seasonal steam train excursions on weekends! Known as "Movie Railroad" — appeared in 200+ films. $5 museum / $20 train ride.

Dog Policy: Dogs on leash allowed throughout the ghost town grounds. Not allowed on Stamp Mill tours or inside the museum.
Activities: ghost town self-guided exploration · history talks (daily at noon) · stamp mill tours · twilight/ghost tours (June–September) · photography · picnicking
Notes: OPEN MAY–OCTOBER ONLY. Best-preserved ghost town in the West. 8375 ft elevation — bring layers. Last 3 mi unpaved but usually passable. No services. Download offline maps!

Dog Policy: No confirmed policy — indoor museum facility. Call ahead: (209) 742-7625.
Activities: mineral and gem exhibit viewing · walk-through mine tunnel replica · stamp mill model viewing · assay office exhibit · junior ranger program · gift shop
Notes: World-class mineral collection. Located at Mariposa County Fairgrounds. Thu–Sun only; winter 10am–4pm / summer 10am–5pm. $4 adults. Combine with Bodie on Labor Day trip.

Top Hike (Name): San Juan Bautista De Anza Trail
Dog Policy: Policy unclear — some sources prohibit all pets; others allow leashed dogs outdoors. Contact park to confirm.
Activities: self-guided historic tours · guided docent tours · living history (1st Saturday monthly) · picnicking · historic adobe and plaza walking · interpretive exhibits
Notes: Only CA mission on its original plaza. Setting of Hitchcock's Vertigo. De Anza Trail is excellent. Fremont Peak SP nearby has camping. Open daily 10am–4:30pm.

Scuba Nearby: YES — Breakwater Cove (beginners); McAbee Beach; Whaler's Cove Point Lobos (advanced); kelp forest / sea lions / wolf eels
Dog Policy: Dogs on leash allowed on paved paths and in picnic areas. Not allowed inside any of the historic park buildings.
Activities: guided walking tours (Thu–Sun) · Path of History self-guided tour · mobile phone audio tour · Stevenson House tour · Larkin House tour · scuba diving · picnicking
Notes: Collection of historic adobes in Old Monterey. California's oldest public buildings. Walk the Path of History. World-class scuba: Breakwater Cove (easy entry/night dives), McAbee Beach, Whaler's Cove at Point Lobos. Best diving Sep–Nov.

Cycling: Bikes allowed on all park trails/roads
Dog Policy: Dogs on leash (max 6 ft) allowed in campgrounds and paved areas. Not allowed inside buildings or on interpretive trails.
Activities: hiking · cycling · self-guided historic town tour · visitor center/museum · picnicking · wildlife viewing · camping · annual Rededication event
Notes: Only CA town founded and governed by African Americans. 15 developed + ~100 tent sites. $20–30/night. Avoid summer (100°F+). En-route stop on SoCal trip ideal.

Dog Policy: Dogs on leash allowed in outdoor areas throughout the park.
Activities: self-guided tours · audio cell phone tours · guided tours (by appointment) · living history demonstrations (1st Saturday monthly) · Civil War reenactments · picnicking · hiking
Notes: 1854 US Army fort. Camel Corps history — camels were used here experimentally. Group camping by reservation only. Grounds accessible daily 9am–4pm. Easy I-5 stop heading south.

Dog Policy: Pets are not allowed on the nature trail or in any park buildings. Only service animals permitted.
Activities: guided museum tours · nature trail hiking · birdwatching · picnicking · Native American cultural programs · living history demonstrations · art demonstrations
Notes: Open SATURDAYS AND SUNDAYS ONLY (11am–4pm). Plan SoCal trip around this park's hours! Museum built into boulders — extraordinary folk architecture. Mojave Desert setting. $3 adults.

Cycling: Lompoc Valley cycling; mountain biking on trails
Scuba Nearby: YES (nearby) — Jalama Beach 15 mi; cold water kelp diving
Dog Policy: Dogs on leash allowed on multipurpose trails and roads. Not allowed inside historic buildings or visitor center. Owners must clean up.
Activities: self-guided mission tour · hiking (25 miles of trails) · mountain biking · garden walking · living history demonstrations · blacksmith shop viewing · picnicking · birdwatching
Notes: Most completely restored CA mission. 2000 acres / 25 miles trails open to hiking AND mountain biking. 9am–5pm daily. Free with state parks pass.

Park Name: El Presidio de Santa Barbara SHP
Cycling: Santa Barbara Waterfront Bike Path (4 mi)
Scuba Nearby: YES — Leadbetter Beach; Anacapa Island (Channel Islands) 12 mi offshore
Dog Policy: Dogs on leash allowed in outdoor/paved areas. Not allowed inside historic buildings or archaeological dig sites.
Activities: self-guided tours · guided tours (weekends) · archaeological site viewing · exhibits and displays · chapel visit · gift shop · Japanese community history exhibit
Notes: 1782 Spanish military presidio in downtown SB. Very small urban site (5.75 acres) but architecturally fascinating. Free. Channel Islands diving nearby is world-class (garibaldi, kelp forests, sea lions).

Cycling: Sepulveda Basin Bike Path (6 mi nearby)
Dog Policy: Dogs on leash allowed in outdoor areas including grounds and pond area. Not allowed inside historic buildings.
Activities: walking/strolling grounds · birdwatching (duck pond) · picnicking · guided adobe tours · historic site viewing (de la Ossa Adobe / Garnier building / blacksmith shop / spring)
Notes: 4.7-acre rancho site in Encino with spring-fed pond. de la Ossa Adobe and Garnier Building. Free entry. Wed–Sun 10am–5pm. Small but a hidden gem among the LA parks.

Scuba Nearby: YES — Will Rogers Beach; Leo Carrillo Beach 15 mi north
Dog Policy: Dogs on leash (max 6 ft) allowed in historic zone and on Inspiration Point Loop. Not allowed on Backbone Trail, Temescal Trail, or Rustic Canyon Trail.
Activities: hiking (Inspiration Point Loop) · polo matches (weekends May–mid Oct free) · horseback riding · equestrian arena use · picnicking · historic ranch house tour · birdwatching
Notes: WARNING: Ranch house and stables DESTROYED in Jan 2025 Palisades Wildfire. Park reopened Nov 2025 with limited access. Verify current status at parks.ca.gov before visiting. Trails and Inspiration Point viewpoint open.

Dog Policy: Dogs allowed on all trails on a max 6-foot leash. Not allowed in the visitor center or museum.
Activities: hiking · grove tours (Fri–Sun at 11am & 2pm) · citrus tastings (Fri–Sun) · picnicking · visitor center/museum · interactive digital tours · special events
Notes: Living heritage citrus grove with 75+ varietals. You can sample fruit while walking! 1 mi east of 91 Fwy. Visitor Center Fri–Sun 10am–4pm. Park open daily 8am–5pm. 15 mph bike limit in park.
